sourceTitle: How to get better answers from Notion AI

questions.question: What style of language does Notion AI work best with when you ask a question?

options.text: Formal, command‑like phrasing
options.explanation: Formal commands are less natural and can limit the AI’s ability to interpret intent.

options.text: Natural, conversational language
options.explanation: The guide recommends using a casual, conversational tone, just as you would speak to a coworker.

options.text: Only short keywords without any context
options.explanation: Pure keyword strings miss the contextual clues that improve relevance.

options.text: Programming syntax or code snippets
options.explanation: Code syntax is not appropriate for general workspace searches.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four recommended dimensions for narrowing Notion AI results?

options.text: Topic
options.explanation: Specifying the subject matter helps focus the search.

options.text: Author
options.explanation: Including the creator’s name narrows the pool of documents.

options.text: Color
options.explanation: Color is not listed as a dimension; the guide mentions topic, author, time context, and document type.

options.text: Document type
options.explanation: Stating whether you need a page, database, etc., is a key dimension.

questions.question: If you receive a list of irrelevant results, what is the first adjustment the guide suggests?

options.text: Switch to formal command language
options.explanation: Formal language isn’t recommended for better relevance.

options.text: Adjust the search scope using the source picker
options.explanation: Changing the source scope helps filter out unrelated content.

options.text: Delete the current conversation and start over
options.explanation: The guide encourages iteration, not restarting.

options.text: Add more emojis to the prompt
options.explanation: Emojis have no impact on search relevance.

questions.question: How does adding a time context improve Notion AI’s search results?

options.text: It tells the AI the document’s creation date, which it can’t see
options.explanation: AI only sees last‑edited timestamps, not creation dates.

options.text: It narrows results to items edited within the specified period
options.explanation: Specifying a timeframe helps the AI focus on recent or relevant versions.

options.text: It changes the formatting of the returned documents
options.explanation: Time context doesn’t affect formatting.

options.text: It makes the AI respond faster
options.explanation: Speed isn’t directly impacted by time qualifiers.

questions.question: When you need a document created by a specific teammate, which technique does the guide recommend?

options.text: Use an @ mention of the person
options.explanation: The guide highlights @ mentions as a quick way to target a specific author.

options.text: Spell their name backwards in the prompt
options.explanation: Backwards spelling provides no benefit.

options.text: Only include the generic term “team update”
options.explanation: A generic term lacks the author dimension.

options.text: Add an emoji next to their name
options.explanation: Emojis do not inform the AI about authorship.

questions.question: If the first AI response isn’t exactly what you need, what should you do next?

options.text: Start a new conversation from scratch
options.explanation: The guide encourages building on the existing context rather than restarting.

options.text: Rephrase the request and add more specific details
options.explanation: Iterative refinement with clearer details leads to better answers.

options.text: Delete the workspace to avoid confusion
options.explanation: Deleting the workspace is unrelated to improving a prompt.

options.text: Change the UI theme to dark mode
options.explanation: Theme changes have no effect on AI output.

questions.question: What does the source picker allow you to control?

options.text: The font style of the returned documents
options.explanation: Font styling is unrelated to source selection.

options.text: Which information sources Notion AI searches
options.explanation: The source picker filters the set of pages, databases, etc., that AI will consider.

options.text: Your account’s storage quota
options.explanation: Storage limits are not managed via the source picker.

options.text: Automatic translation of content
options.explanation: Translation isn’t a function of the source picker.

questions.question: How can you broaden the timeframe of a Notion AI search?

options.text: Specify older years or a wider date range in the prompt
options.explanation: Explicitly mentioning a larger period tells the AI to include older content.

options.text: Ask only for the “latest” documents
options.explanation: That narrows, not broadens, the timeframe.

options.text: Remove any time references entirely
options.explanation: Removing time context may default to a recent look‑back window, limiting older results.

options.text: Add a smiley face after the request
options.explanation: Emojis don’t affect the temporal scope.

questions.question: Why does the guide suggest mixing dimensions instead of always including all four?

options.text: Using all dimensions at once can overwhelm the AI and reduce relevance
options.explanation: The guide warns that over‑specifying may confuse the model, so start with the most important dimensions and add more as needed.

options.text: Including every dimension guarantees a perfect result every time
options.explanation: Even with full detail, the AI might still need refinement; over‑loading isn’t a guarantee of perfection.

options.text: Mixing dimensions speeds up the AI’s processing time
options.explanation: Speed isn’t the primary concern; relevance is.

options.text: Dimensions are only useful for search, not for content creation
options.explanation: Dimensions help both searching and generating content by providing context.

questions.question: If Notion AI seems to answer with general knowledge instead of searching your workspace, how should you clarify your intent?

options.text: Add a phrase like “search my workspace for …”
options.explanation: Explicitly stating that you want a workspace search directs the AI away from generic answers.

options.text: Write the request in all caps
options.explanation: Capitalization does not change the AI’s scope interpretation.

options.text: Include a smiley emoji at the end
options.explanation: Emojis do not affect the AI’s decision to search versus answer from its knowledge base.

options.text: Change the Notion theme to dark mode
options.explanation: Theme settings are unrelated to search scope.
